content.problemStatement:
Today is the final round of La Liga, the most popular professional football league in the world. Real Madrid is playing against Malaga and Barcelona is playing against Eibar. These two matches will decide who wins the league title. Real Madrid is already 3 points ahead of Barcelona in the league standings. In fact, Real Madrid will win the league title, except for one scenario: If Real Madrid loses against Malaga, and Barcelona wins against Eibar, then the La Liga title will go to Barcelona. In any other combination of results, Real Madrid will win the title.

You will be given multiple scenarios for these two games, where in each one you will be given the number of goals each team scored in their respective match. A team wins a match if it scores more than the opponent. In case they score the same number of goals, it's a draw. Otherwise, the team loses the game. You are asked to tell the winner of the La Liga title in each scenario.
constraints: 0 <= number of goals scored by a team in a match <= 20
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single number T, the number of scenarios. Each scenario is described by four lines. Each line starts with a team name followed by the number of goals this team scored in its corresponding match.
content.outputFormat: For each scenario, output a single line showing the title winner in case this scenario happens. It should be either 'RealMadrid' or 'Barcelona'.

sampleTestCases.input:
2
Barcelona 2
Malaga 1
RealMadrid 1
Eibar 0
Malaga 3
RealMadrid 2
Barcelona 8
Eibar 6
sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first scenario, Real Madrid wins because it does not lose to Malaga and Barcelona does not win against Eibar. In the second scenario, Barcelona wins because Real Madrid loses to Malaga and Barcelona wins against Eibar.
Real Madrid wins because it wins against Malaga and Barcelona does not win against Eibar.
Barcelona wins because Real Madrid loses to Malaga and Barcelona wins against Eibar.

solution.approach: The approach to solve this problem is to iterate over each scenario, compare the scores of the two matches, and determine the winner based on the given conditions. If Real Madrid loses and Barcelona wins, then Barcelona is the winner. Otherwise, Real Madrid is the winner.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — the time complexity is linear with respect to the number of scenarios, as we are iterating over each scenario once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant, as we are not using any additional data structures that scale with the input size.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    team1, score1 = input().split()
    score1 = int(score1)
    team2, score2 = input().split()
    score2 = int(score2)
    team3, score3 = input().split()
    score3 = int(score3)
    team4, score4 = input().split()
    score4 = int(score4)
    if team1 == 'RealMadrid' and score1 < score2 and team3 == 'Barcelona' and score3 > score4:
        print('Barcelona')
    else:
        print('RealMadrid')
